scenario.summary: Weekly scan — 6 flagged anomalies across AP, AR, GL, and intercompany
scenario.situation: Continuous monitoring mode. The Internal Auditor reviews the weekly anomaly report. The system scans all transactions across AP, AR, GL, and intercompany — looking for patterns that rules-based systems miss. This week, the agent flagged 6 items across 3 entities. The auditor needs to triage: which are real risks, which are false positives, and which need immediate escalation.
dataInputs: Full transaction log across all processes and entities
agentSteps: Scans 42,000 transactions from the past week across all entities and processes
Critical (95%): INV-88206 — duplicate invoice from GlobalSteel, same PO, same amount, 2 days apart. Vendor has had 3 duplicate submissions in the past 12 months (pattern, not accident)

High (88%): Vendor 8832 — new vendor onboarded in Houston with a $95K invoice, no vendor approval workflow completed, bank account in a different state than registered address. Recommends hold and vendor verification
High (82%): Manual JE-9010 — $1.2M manual journal entry posted Nov 29 with no supporting documentation, posting user doesn't normally create entries of this size. Recommends immediate review
Medium (71%): JE-9005 — JPY 12M FX revaluation is 3x historical average. Confirmed as legitimate but unusual. Recommends hedge review
Low (45%): AR-4372 — Pacific Dynamics 31 days overdue, payment silence combined with credit downgrade triggers risk alert
Low (32%): Unusual spike in Summit Logistics invoices (12 in November vs. average of 8). Likely seasonal but flagged for awareness
scenario.expectedOutput: Weekly anomaly report with 6 flagged items, confidence scores, evidence trails, and recommended actions. 2 items recommended for immediate escalation (vendor 8832, JE-9010), 1 confirmed by another agent (duplicate invoice), 3 for monitoring.
